name: Barbecued Pig's Tails
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
We attribute this recipe to the Mennonite colony of Ontario. It's often served at picnics and is known as one of the favorite foods at stag parties.
1. Prepare pigs' tails according to Step I in basic instructions; cut into 3-inch pieces.
2. Place pigs' tails on a rack and bake in a 300°F oven for 2 to 3 hours, until meat is tender and most of the fat has baked off. Alternatively, simmer the pigs' tails in acidulated water for 1½ hours.
3. Combine all other ingredients for barbecue sauce, coating tails well.
4. Grill over hot charcoal, turning and basting, until crisp. If fresh rosemary is available, break off several branches, tie them together, and use as a basting brush. For barbecuing, leaving the tails uncut or halved results in fewer pieces to constantly turn.
5. Serve with traditional barbecue accompaniments such as corn-on-the-cob, French bread, and green salad.
Serves 6 to 8.
